address: Zaya Hameni — Unit E706, JVC District 15, Dubai (For Rent)

rent_snapshot.verdict: Rent verdict: Fair market rent for E706 is ~AED 128,000/yr. Anchor your offer at the DLD index and target AED 120,000–125,000 unfurnished (low-mid floor = leverage). A softening, supply-heavy 2026 market favours the tenant. Treat any asking price of AED 150,000+ as overpriced — that reflects the building's larger 1,866–3,138 sqft units, not this 1,404 sqft layout. Furnished adds ~AED 10,000–15,000.

investment_metrics.cap_rate_status: Gross yield 6.6%; net slightly below Dubai 5-7% norm (service-charge drag)
investment_metrics.coc_status: Cash buyer; positive and stable. ~Neutral at 80% LTV
investment_metrics.grm_status: Price-to-annual-rent; typical for premium Dubai towers
investment_metrics.dscr_status: Cash-flow-neutral at 80% LTV / ~4.4% rate — larger deposit advised
investment_metrics.one_pct_status: Below 1% rule — normal for Dubai capital-growth markets

details.notes: Dubai low-crime; main issue is construction noise

demographics.major_employers: Media City, Internet City, Marina, Business Bay (nearby)

strategies.pros: Tax-free income + appreciation; high liquidity in Dubai's #1-volume district
strategies.risk: Thin leveraged cash flow; 2026 supply may soften rents

strategies.pros: Push rent toward 135k+ ceiling; modest resale premium
strategies.risk: Capped upside; many units already well-finished

scenarios.trigger: Population surge continues, Blue Line momentum, supply absorbed
Normalisation: steady prices, flat-to-soft rents as 2026 stock lands
Oversupply outpaces demand, EIBOR rises above 5%, rent discounts deepen

recommendation.summary: For a tenant, E706 is a RENT-SMART opportunity: fair rent is ~AED 128,000/yr, so anchor negotiations at the DLD index and target AED 120,000-125,000 unfurnished — a softening, supply-heavy market gives you leverage, and any 150k+ ask (which reflects the building's larger 1,866-3,138 sqft units) is overpriced for this 1,404 sqft layout. For an investor, it is a sound Grade-A buy-and-hold at ≤ AED 1.90M: tax-free ~6.6% gross income, deep tenant demand in Dubai's highest-volume community, and appreciation tailwinds (Hessa St upgrade, 2029 Blue Line Metro). Net yield (~4.5%) is capped by premium service charges, and leveraged cash flow is thin at current rates — so cash or a larger deposit and a 5+ year horizon are key.
action_items: Verify the exact RERA service charge for Tower E (biggest yield variable)
Confirm furnished vs unfurnished and cheque count before negotiating rent
Check the DLD/RERA rental index calculator for the legal renewal cap
Inspect immediate surroundings for active construction / noise near Tower E
If buying: stress-test the mortgage at +1% EIBOR and confirm the reserve fund

risk_factors.notes: JVC top oversupply-watch zone; softens rents and compresses yields toward 6-7%
~AED 21k/yr (~16.5% of rent) is the single biggest yield killer — verify Tower E figure
Cash flow turns negative if rates >5% or rent sits at the 117k floor
~8-9% round-trip; any sub-3-year hold is risky — buy-to-let, not a flip
Newer JVC pockets face 1-2 more years of disruption; inspect nearby plots
